Title: Security, human rights top Asean topics during 59th AMM and related meetings
Article:
MANILA: The Philippines is set to lead critical discussions on regional security, human rights, and geopolitical stability as it hosts the 59th Asean Foreign Ministers' Meeting (AMM) and Related Meetings from July 20 to 24, the Philippine News Agency (PNA) reported.
In a press briefing on Monday, Foreign Affairs Assistant Secretary and Asean Spokesperson Dominic Xavier Imperial said the high-level gathering will focus on advancing key development priorities and reinforcing the regional bloc's community identity amid rising global tensions.
"Building on the momentum we have sustained over the past seven months, these meetings will advance key development priorities and further reinforce Asean community identity," he said.
The week-long activities commenced with the 16th face-to-face interface between Asean Foreign Ministers and representatives of the Asean Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights (AICHR).
"The AMM-AICHR interface underscores the commitment of Asean towards advancing the promotion and protection of human rights and fundamental freedoms for all peoples of the region, especially those in vulnerable situations," Imperial said.
Regional ministers convened for the Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone (SEANWFZ) Commission meeting to review the 2023–2027 plan of action and deliberate on the accession of new candidate states to its Protocol.
The longstanding objective remains securing global recognition and support for keeping Southeast Asia entirely free of nuclear arms.
The formal 59th AMM opens on Tuesday with the foreign ministers, diplomatic corps members, and resident missions in the Philippines attending the external session.
The body is expected to review progress across Asean's three community pillars, evaluate external relations, and exchange views on pressing international issues.
The ministers are expected to adopt two pivotal outcome documents, which include the 59th AMM Joint Communiqué and the Guidelines on the Modalities for Engagement of Asean with High Contracting Parties to the Treaty of Amity and Cooperation in Southeast Asia.
The communiqué is a comprehensive document reflecting regional decisions, positions on international developments, and core priorities.
The guidelines are a key deliverable of the Philippine chairmanship, establishing a structured framework for states that lack official partnerships with Asean.
"The guidelines are intended to strengthen cooperation with these states while upholding the principles of the TAC as the cornerstone of peace, stability, and confidence-building in the region," Imperial said.
On Wednesday, Asean will hold its Post-Ministerial Conferences (PMCs) with key dialogue partners, including India, the United States, Canada, Russia, China, the Republic of Korea, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, and the European Union.
On Thursday, the 27th Asean Plus Three (APT) meeting, the 33rd Asean Regional Forum (ARF), and the 16th East Asia Summit (EAS) will follow.
Amid heightened worldwide tensions, Imperial highlighted the significance of these multilateral engagements.
"This is a very good opportunity, a venue for the dialogue partners... to address whatever is impacting currently regionally as well as globally", he said.
A highly anticipated bilateral meeting is also confirmed between Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi.
"We believe that this engagement is very important because we will address what we want to cover in bilateral relations between the Philippines and China," he said.
"It also has something to do with our regional impact of what's happening globally to the region."
The ongoing crises in Myanmar and territorial disputes in the South China Sea are slated to dominate the ministerial discussions.
While exact negotiating positions remain fluid, Imperial confirmed that the Code of Conduct (COC) in the South China Sea and the enforcement of the Five-Point Consensus on Myanmar are permanent fixtures on the agenda.
"On the COC, it will always be a part of their discussions. And also on Myanmar, there will be an exchange of views on this issue," he said.
He added, "Engagement continues with not only the [State Administration Council] but also with other Myanmar stakeholders" to achieve measurable progress.
The week will conclude on Friday with a commemorative activity marking the TAC's 50th anniversary.
The landmark event features a signing ceremony for new acceding states, the inaugural high-level conference of the High Contracting Parties, and a gala dinner hosted by President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN, BN machinery cooperation smooth in Negri Sembilan campaign
Article:
SEREMBAN: Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) campaign machinery have been operating smoothly, with grassroots coordination remaining positive, Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said.
Tuan Ibrahim, who is also PN deputy chairman, said most campaign activities were focused on each candidate's respective constituencies, although several joint programmes involving BN and PN were also being carried out.
"So far, coordination has taken place at the constituency and polling district unit levels, and the overall cooperation has been fairly positive," he said when contacted.
Tuan Ibrahim said there had been no significant resistance to the cooperation between the two coalitions.
"Although some state constituencies contested by PN are facing challenges from other parties, particularly Bersatu, these are not considered a major threat," he said.
He said adverse weather conditions had slowed the pace of campaigning, prompting party machinery to focus on house-to-house visits, voter outreach and smaller closed-door engagements instead of large-scale events.
Despite the conditions, Tuan Ibrahim said ceramah continued to draw encouraging crowds.
Yesterday, Parti Wawasan Negara president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin said PN and BN would combine their election machinery for the Negri Sembilan election.
He said the two coalitions had begun showing a willingness to work together at all levels ahead of the state election.
Negri Sembilan will go to the polls on Aug 1, with BN contesting 25 of the 36 seats and PN the remaining 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court: Zizie had no knowledge of Felcra investment, acquitted
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court has found former actress Datin Seri Zizie Izette A. Samad to be a credible witness, ruling that her testimony was consistent with the prosecution's evidence that she did not abet her late husband in obtaining RM2.8 million in alleged bribes.
Judge Rosli Ahmad said the defence had established that Zizie had no knowledge of Felcra Berhad's RM150 million investment in Public Mutual unit trust products and was never involved in her late husband's official duties as Felcra's former non-executive chairman.
Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, the former Kinabatangan MP, died on Dec 5 last year.
In his broad grounds of judgment, Rosli said the defence relied on the evidence of Zizie, Bung Moktar and former Felcra group chief financial officer Mohd Azmy Abu Samad.
He said the evidence clearly showed that Zizie's role was limited to introducing Public Mutual investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid (PW24) and unit trust consultant Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar (PW25) to Bung Moktar and Azmy.
"The accused did not expect PW24 and PW25 to pay her an introduction fee following their meeting with the deceased.
"The introduction fee was given voluntarily.
"The accused did not inform the deceased that she had received the introduction fee, and the deceased had no knowledge of it.
"The accused did not interfere in the deceased's official duties or financial affairs and had no knowledge of Felcra Berhad's investment in Public Mutual unit trust products," he said.
Rosli said Zizie denied assisting Bung Moktar in obtaining bribes from Madhi and Norhaili, as alleged in the three amended charges.
"She also denied abetting the deceased in obtaining approval from Felcra Berhad or the second finance minister for Felcra Berhad to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual unit trust products. The accused never asked the deceased about Felcra Berhad's affairs.
"The accused's testimony was consistent, reasonable and supported by the evidence of other witnesses and documents tendered in court.
"This evidence proved that the accused did not abet the deceased in obtaining bribes from Madhi and Norhaili," he said.
On Bung Moktar's evidence, Rosli said the deceased testified that he had never discussed Felcra's official matters with Zizie.
"He further testified that he had never requested or received any money the accused received from Madhi and Norhaili, as he was never involved in her financial affairs," he said.
Rosli said Bung Moktar was acquitted following the prosecution's application.
"As a result, the deceased is regarded as an innocent man because no finding of guilt was recorded against him.
"In other words, the prosecution failed to prove beyond reasonable doubt that the deceased had received bribes.
"The discharge and acquittal affected an essential element of the abetment charge against the accused, namely that the principal offender had committed the corruption offence.
"Where the principal offender is discharged and acquitted, an accused alleged to have abetted that offence cannot also be convicted.
"This court is of the view that the prosecution failed to prove its case against the accused beyond reasonable doubt," he said.
Zizie was represented by Datuk Seri K. Kumaraendran, Datuk M. Athimulan, Ridha Abdah Subri and Teh See Khoon. Deputy public prosecutors Law Chin How and Mohamad Fadhly Mohd Zamry appeared for the prosecution.
After the proceedings, Fadhly said the prosecution would await the Attorney-General's decision on whether to appeal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court told RM8mil transferred from FashionValet to 30 Maple
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court here was told today that RM8 million was transferred from FashionValet Sdn Bhd's bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd through two online transactions in August 2018.
CIMB Bank's Taman Tun Dr Ismail branch customer service manager Azizan Hassan, 45, testified that a review of 30 Maple's bank statements revealed online deposits of RM5 million and RM3 million from FashionValet's account.
"On Aug 21, 2018, there was a deposit of RM5 million from FashionValet Sdn Bhd with the transaction description, 'REMITTANCE CR/ROC/PB ECP PAYMENT FASHIONVALET SDN BHD PB ECP PAYMENT FASHION VALET SDN BHD ADVANCES TO 30 MAPLE'.
"I also confirm that the account balance before the deposit was RM2,678,853.48.
"On the same day, there was another deposit of RM3 million with a similar transaction description from FashionValet. I also confirm that the account balance before the RM3 million deposit was RM7,678,853.48," she told the court.
Azizan was reading her witness statement on the opening day of the trial involving FashionValet founders Datin Vivy Yusof and her husband, Datuk Fadzarudin Shah
Anuar, who are facing charges of criminal breach of trust involving RM8 million in investment funds from Khazanah Nasional Bhd (Khazanah) and Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB).
The prosecution's first witness also told the court that 30 Maple's bank statements showed a withdrawal of RM10,213,537.28 from the company's account on Sept 3, 2018.
She further testified that the same account recorded an inflow of RM5 million on Sept 3, followed by a RM5 million withdrawal on Sept 4, 2018, bearing the transaction description, "Interim Dividend FADZARUDIN SHAH".
Azizan was giving evidence during examination-in-chief by deputy public prosecutors Noor Syazwani Mohamad Sobry and Haresh Prakash Somiah.
She also confirmed that Vivy and Fadzarudin, who were both directors of 30 Maple, were the authorised signatories for the company's bank account.
Earlier, lawyer Datuk M. Athimulan, representing the couple, informed Judge Rosli Ahmad that he would only be able to cross-examine the witness on Friday as he was on medical leave until Wednesday.
The court allowed the application.
Vivy, whose full name is Vivy Sofinas Yusof, 38, and Fadzarudin, 37, are jointly charged with committing criminal breach of trust, in their capacity as directors of FashionValet Sdn Bhd, by causing RM8 million in investment funds from Khazanah and PNB to be transferred from FashionValet's bank account to 30 Maple without the approval of FashionValet's board of directors.
The offence was allegedly committed on Aug 21, 2018, at Public Bank Berhad's Bukit Damansara branch.
They are charged under Section 409 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years, whipping and a possible fine upon conviction.
They also pleaded not guilty to an alternative charge of dishonest misappropriation of property belonging to FashionValet, namely Khazanah and PNB investment funds amounting to RM8 million, at the same place and date.
The charge, under Section 403 of the Penal Code, carries a jail term of not less than six months and a maximum of five years, with whipping and a fine upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Timeline on Zizie Izette RM2.8mil corruption abetment case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Actress-turned-businesswoman Datin Seri Zizie Izette A. Samad was acquitted earlier today of three charges of abetting her late husband Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin in receiving RM2.8 million in bribes linked to a RM150 million unit trust investment by Felcra Bhd.
Sessions Court judge Rosli Ahmad ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove its case beyond reasonable doubt,
freeing the 48-year-old widow
of the former Kinabatangan member of parliament.
The case centred on three charges alleging that Zizie abetted Bung Moktar in receiving cash bribes as an inducement to secure approval from then-Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Ahmad Husni Mohamad Hanadzlah for Felcra to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual Bhd unit trust products.
TIMELINE
June 12 and 19, 2015: Alleged transactions take place at a Public Bank Bhd branch in Taman Melawati, involving cash bribes totalling RM2.8 million routed through accounts registered in Zizie's name.
May 3, 2019
: Bung Moktar is charged with two counts of corruptly receiving RM2.2 million and RM262,500 linked to Felcra's unit trust investment. A third charge alleges he received RM337,500 from Public Mutual Bhd agent Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar through Zizie and accounts in her name.
Sept 2, 2022
: Sessions Court finds prosecution has established a prima facie case and orders Bung Moktar and Zizie to enter their defence.
Sept 7, 2023
: High Court overturns Sessions Court's ruling, acquitting both.
Nov 18, 2024
: Court of Appeal allows the prosecution's appeal against High Court acquittal, reinstating the order for both to enter their defence.
Sept 2, 2025
: Bung Moktar takes the stand in his defence.
Dec 5, 2025
: Bung Moktar dies.
Jan 16, 2026
: Sessions Court acquits Bung Moktar of all three charges after the prosecution opts not to continue the case following his death.
March 5, 2026
: Zizie concludes her testimony, after which the defence closes its case.
July 20, 2026
: Sessions Court acquits Zizie of all three charges, with judge Rosli Ahmad finding the prosecution had failed to prove its case beyond reasonable doubt.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Last of Mexico's narco old guard, 'El Mayo' faces life in jail
Article:
MEXICO CITY: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, founding leader of the legendary Sinaloa cartel, will be sentenced in New York on Monday, a ruling expected to send Mexico's last old-school druglord to prison for life.
After a journey from poor farmworker to one of the world's most powerful and wealthy criminal bosses, Zambada was arrested at a US airport in July 2024.
He claims he was tricked by a cartel ally into boarding a plane that delivered him into the hands of US authorities.
Known for keeping a low profile and avoiding being photographed, Zambada, now 76 years old, spent decades rising through the cartel ranks, evading both capture and multiple assassination attempts.
He was born in 1950 in Sinaloa — a rugged mountainous state of Mexico known for producing corn, beans, cattle, and some of the world's most notorious drug traffickers.
His early years coincided with the emergence of Sinaloa as a marijuana and opium growing region — and eventually a transit point for Colombian cocaine going to the United States.
Mexico's drug violence has surged since then.
Since 2006, almost half a million people have been killed, and 130,000 are missing, according to government estimates.
- 'Systematic assassinations' -
Zambada began his criminal career with the Guadalajara and Juarez cartels, working alongside Miguel Angel Felix Gallardo and "The Lord of the Skies" Amado Carrillo Fuentes — both also from Sinaloa.
But he eventually struck out, working alongside another Sinaloan, Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman, to establish the Sinaloa cartel in the 1980s and 1990s.
With Zambada playing a key role in paying off officials, setting up distribution networks across the United States, and moving billions of dollars back to Mexico, the Sinaloa cartel soon became the world's largest.
It is one of six Mexican drug gangs that US President Donald Trump has designated as Foreign Terrorist Organisations.
US Court documents paint him as not just smart, but violently ruthless.
He allegedly ordered the killing of his own nephew, who was then found dead in a car by the side of the road.
According to US prosecutors, Zambada also engaged in "systematic assassinations" of fellow cartel members, police, and the military.
In 2010, he gave a rare interview to the Mexican magazine Proceso, revealing that the Mexican army had come close to capturing him four times.
Known as the "man in the hat", Zambada said that he feared jail more than death, and his arrest or murder would not change Mexico's drug trafficking landscape.
Almost a year after being detained in the United States, he pleaded guilty to drug trafficking and organised crime charges.
- $15 billion forfeited -
His former partner Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman is already serving a life sentence in the United States.
El Chapo's demise prompted an internal war within the Sinaloa cartel between Zambada's supporters and El Chapo's sons, which has left about 2,000 people dead.
El Chapo's son, Joaquin Guzman Lopez, was on the same plane that brought Zambada to the United States.
Lopez was arrested and admitted to having kidnapped Zambada as part of his own plea deal.
The operation continues to raise questions nearly two years later, as well as tension between Mexico and the United States over the FBI's alleged participation.
The United States has denied any role in the operation to bring Zambada onto US soil.
However, the recent display of the aircraft that brought Zambada to the United States as part of an FBI operations collection prompted Mexico to seek explanations.
A court in Brooklyn is expected to sentence him on Monday.
Under his plea deal with US authorities, Zambada faced a mandatory minimum term of life imprisonment without parole on a racketeering charge.
He also agreed to the forfeiture of $15 billion of ill-gotten-gains as part of the plea deal, the Department of Justice said. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indian police baton charge protesters marching to parliament
Article:
NEW DELHI: India's riot police baton-charged protesters in New Delhi as they tried to march to parliament on Monday, escalating a protest over irregularities in major examinations.
Protesters ran for cover as police in blue riot gear charged at them near the Jantar Mantar protest site, an AFP journalist saw.
Thousands of protesters started gathering from early Monday, defying a steady monsoon drizzle and police barricades put up on routes leading to the site.
The protesters are demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the irregularities, including question paper leaks and technical glitches.
"We want the government to hear us, not silence us," said student K.M. Gulshan, 19.
The protest comes after prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk was taken to hospital on Saturday, bringing an abrupt end to his 20-day hunger strike in support of youths and the satirical Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) movement.
The CJP has won millions of followers on social media since its launch in May, tapping into widespread anger over the education system and unemployment.
The movement is seen as one of the biggest challenges to Hindu-nationalist Prime Minister Narendra Modi since he won his third term in office in 2024.
"PM @narendramodi tell your police to stand down," the CJP said in a post on X on Monday.
"We are a democracy in case you have forgotten. The whole world is watching as you're trying to crush a democratic and peaceful protest."
Despite rapid economic growth, millions of people in the world's most populous nation still struggle to find stable and well-paying jobs, fuelling discontent.
Some 2.2 million aspiring medical students sat for a re-examination under tight security last month after the previous test was scrapped following a question paper leak that triggered widespread outrage.
That came on top of another scandal related to the online marking system of tests taken by nearly two million high school students.
The Internet was snapped in parts of central New Delhi on Monday, with police saying the protesters did not have their permission to hold the protest.
"Any person violating these prohibitory orders shall be liable for prosecution," Delhi Police said in a statement.
Shubhi Rao, 16, said she had joined the protest to support Wangchuk and demand accountability from the government.
"Prime Minister Narendra Modi has shown himself as someone who isn't bothered about the youth of this country," she told AFP.
Modi, in a public address before the start of the monsoon session of parliament, did not mention the protesters.
But Mallikarjun Kharge, a senior leader of the opposition Congress party, brought up the issue in the upper house.
"Thousands of students have gathered at Jantar Mantar. The government is trying to use force, suppress voices, and drive them away," he said.
Slogans were also shouted inside the lower house by the opposition benches, demanding the resignation of the education minister and justice for students.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ryanair quarterly profit slumps by a third on lower fares, higher fuel costs
Article:
DUBLIN: Ryanair's profit slumped by a third on higher fuel costs and weaker fares in the three months to the end of June, the airline said on Monday, while summer fares looked set to fall amid consumer nervousness around the Iran war and broader economy.
The weak results for Ryanair, Europe's largest airline by passenger numbers, are the latest sign that the five-month-old Iran war is being felt more keenly by companies as peace talks drag and oil prices remain elevated.
"The price of our 20 per cent unhedged fuel doubled in the quarter and fares fell si per cent, primarily we think due to the impact of the Middle East conflict" and the timing of Easter, chief executive Michael O'Leary said in a video presentation.
"Despite a recent, slight, uptick in volumes, and less price stimulation, Q2 pricing is trending modestly down year-on-year," he added.
At its last quarterly results in May, Ryanair said fares could be broadly flat between July and September.
The Irish airline reported after-tax profit of €538 million (US$616 million) for its fiscal first quarter through June 30, down 34 per cent from the previous year and short of a forecast of €579 million in a company poll of analysts.
Citi analysts said in a note that the airline's shares looked likely to open down by a low- to mid-single-digit percentage following the miss.
CFO SEES CAPACITY FALLS, FARE INCREASES IN COMING YEAR
Weakness in fares is likely to be short-lived, however, as European aviation is facing a wave of consolidation and airline failures that will take out capacity, chief financial officer Neil Sorahan said.
"I wouldn't be surprised to see a number of casualties this winter ... there's a few people very much on the edge," Sorahan said in an interview.
He said he expected "significant capacity" to be cut in Europe this winter, "which could be positive for pricing," and a lot more may be taken out in summer 2027.
The possible sale of British rival easyJet, which is the subject of a bidding war, could also lead to a reduction in capacity and could trigger a "domino effect" of consolidation in Europe, Sorahan said.
Ryanair has not seen any hit to bookings from the heatwaves that have hit Europe in recent months, Sorahan added.
"We're still flying full loads down to the Mediterranean every day, down to Greece and elsewhere. People as keen to get away as ever, albeit booking just a little bit later," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops probe fatal four-vehicle crash, father riding with three children
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police will conduct a thorough investigation into a four-vehicle crash that claimed the life of a factory operator after she was thrown from a van and crushed beneath the overturned vehicle at Jalan Permas Utara here this morning.
Johor Baru South police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said
investigators would also examine
the circumstances surrounding a motorcyclist who was travelling with his three young children at the time of the crash.
The 37-year-old man and his children, aged 9, 7 and 5, sustained minor injuries after their motorcycle collided with a Perodua Alza.
The crash, which occurred at 7.15am, involved a Honda Civic, a van, the Alza and the motorcycle. All four vehicles were travelling towards the city centre.
Initial investigations found that the Honda Civic, driven by a 29-year-old woman, allegedly lost control before veering into the opposite lane.
"The Honda, travelling from Permas City towards Kota Masai, collided with a van driven by a 39-year-old man.
"Following the impact, the van spun into the opposite lane before overturning," Azrul said in a statement today.
The impact caused the van's 42-year-old female passenger, a factory operator, to be thrown from the vehicle through the left-side door before she became trapped beneath it.
She suffered severe head injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene by paramedics.
Her body was sent to Sultan Ismail Hospital for a post-mortem.
The Honda driver, her male passenger and the van driver escaped unhurt.
The 46-year-old woman driving the Perodua Alza sustained minor injuries to her neck, cheek and mouth.
The motorcyclist and his three children crashed into the Alza while attempting to avoid the pile-up.
All injured victims were treated at Sultan Ismail Hospital.
Azrul said the case was being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
He urged witnesses to contact investigating officer Inspector Dayang Aziemah Awang Sabeli at 017-3064779 or the Johor Baru South Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division.
He also reminded motorists to remain vigilant, obey traffic laws and ensure their vehicles were roadworthy to help prevent accidents and loss of life.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cyprus marks 52 years since invasion as UN renews peace push
Article:
NICOSIA: Cyprus marked the 52-year anniversary of Turkiye's 1974 invasion on Monday as the Greek and Turkish Cypriot communities prepared for a renewed effort from the United Nations to break years of deadlock in peace talks.
Air raid sirens sounded over the Southern Greek Cypriot government-controlled areas of Cyprus on Monday morning to commemorate the hour Turkish forces landed in 1974, days after a short-lived Greek-inspired coup.
Military celebrations were planned on Monday in the Turkish Cypriot north, a breakaway state recognised only by Ankara.
U.N Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit from July 27 to 29 in an attempt to revive a peace process that has produced no substantive negotiations for years.
"We hope the conditions will be created for the resumption of negotiations," Cyprus President Nikos Christodoulides said.
Peace efforts are hampered by disagreement over its intended outcome.
Greek Cypriots seek reunification as a federation, while Turkiye and the Turkish Cypriot leadership seek two sovereign states.
The dispute is a source of tension between Nato partners Greece and Turkiye, and is an obstacle to Ankara's ambitions of joining the European Union, of which Cyprus has been a member since 2004.
The 1974 invasion followed years of intercommunal friction and violence shortly after Cyprus won independence from Britain.
Turkiye describes the invasion as a 'peace operation' undertaken to protect Turkish Cypriots.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MN Holdings secures SC approval for Main Market transfer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MN Holdings Bhd has received the Securities Commission's approval to transfer its listing from the ACE Market to the Main Market of Bursa Malaysia.
Managing director Datuk Clement Toh described the move as a significant milestone for the group, reflecting its stronger financial performance and expanded project portfolio.
"Over the years, we have strengthened our financial performance, expanded our project portfolio, and enhanced our capabilities in delivering critical power and utilities infrastructure projects," he said in a statement.
The approval, granted in a letter dated July 16, is expected to enhance MN Holdings' corporate profile and improve its visibility among a broader base of institutional and retail investors.
Toh said the transfer puts MN Holdings in a stronger position to capitalise on Malaysia's continued investment in power infrastructure, data centres, renewable energy, water, gas and utilities-related developments.
The company has secured a series of contracts this year involving high-voltage consumer landing stations, substation equipment and related power infrastructure works across central and southern Peninsular Malaysia.
Beyond data centre-related projects, it has also expanded its presence in other infrastructure segments.
In June, MN Holdings secured a RM19.1 million extension of works from Tenaga Nasional Bhd for the 132-kilovolt main intake substation project in Masjid Tanah, followed by the award of a RM46.6 million solar farm project in July.
At 12.30pm, MN Holdings shares rose two sen or 0.7 per cent to RM2.86, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM1.91 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Sometimes when it touches conscience, I speak out,' says Anwar
Article:
KAJANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has apologised if his tone while reprimanding civil servants came across as harsh.
He stressed that his remarks stemmed from his concern to ensure the country's agenda could be implemented effectively.
Anwar said he had often been advised to speak more calmly and engage with the public in a more approachable manner after assuming the post of Prime Minister.
However, he said there were times when he needed to express his views firmly, especially on matters involving conscience and responsibility towards the nation.
"I apologise if my tone was slightly raised. Many people have told me, now that you are prime minister, you cannot raise your voice.
"You have to speak calmly, speak well and engage with the people.
"I try, but sometimes when it touches matters of conscience, I speak out. I apologise if that is not befitting of a prime minister.
"I do not know, perhaps a prime minister is expected to always be calm. No, no," he said.
He made the remarks during an engagement session with civil servants from the Central Zone at Dewan Sultan Idris Shah, Universiti Islam Selangor, here today.
Anwar said his remarks were not intended to scold civil servants, but were made because he viewed them as an important part of the machinery driving the country's development agenda.
"If it is considered my mistake, I apologise. But believe me, I am expressing what I feel.
"Who else can I share these concerns with if not my own team, the machinery that can help implement the country's programmes?" he said.
Also present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service Department director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz and Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sonam Wangchuk, Indian activist rallying Gen Z against Modi
Article:
INDIAN social activist Sonam Wangchuk may be pushing 60, but he has become the face of a Gen Z movement demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's education minister over exam paper leaks, drawing wider support after he was forcibly taken to hospital following three weeks of fasting.
Wangchuk, who is widely believed to have inspired the lead character in a Bollywood blockbuster, is best known for campaigning on issues affecting his Himalayan region of Ladakh, bordering China.
But his fast over exam paper leaks that affected millions of students has won him a new following among young Indians, helping turn the issue into a broader Gen Z movement and piling pressure on Modi's government.
Even before police in Delhi moved him to hospital at the weekend after his hunger strike in solidarity with protesting supporters of the youth Cockroach Janta Party, some were comparing Wangchuk to India's independence hero Mahatma Gandhi.
He has been getting video and text messages of support from across India, including from Bollywood actors.
"It pains me when some say you're the Gandhi of the 21st century, the modern Gandhi, you're the real hero," he said this month from Delhi's Jantar Mantar protest site.
"I am no Gandhi, no hero. I'm just an ordinary citizen who has not shied away from his responsibilities. Don't look for a hero in others. Be the hero of your own life and fulfil your responsibilities."
Lovingly called "Sonam Sir" — Sonam means "merit" or "virtue" in some cultures — by many, including CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke, Wangchuk is one of Ladakh's most prominent public figures, having built a career around education reform and water-conservation projects in the high-altitude desert.
In 2018, he was awarded the Ramon Magsaysay Award, widely known as Asia's Nobel Prize, for those efforts.
Born in 1966 in the small, remote village of Ulaytokpo in Ladakh, he was a 19-year-old engineering student at the government-run National Institute of Technology in Kashmir when he ran a coaching programme in basic subjects such as English and mathematics that helped hundreds of students.
He later set up the Students' Educational and Cultural Movement of Ladakh.
As climate change affected water supplies, Wangchuk built artificial glaciers in the form of "ice stupas", conically shaped ice mountains that store water in winter and melt in summer to supply irrigation water, his Magsaysay citation says.
In recent years, he has become a leading advocate for environmental protection and greater autonomy for Ladakh, a federally controlled territory.
He was jailed for nearly six months in 2025 after deadly protests demanding statehood for the region. Wangchuk denied instigating the violence.
Indian media reported that his father, Sonam Wangyal, was a minister in the former state of Jammu and Kashmir and that former Prime Minister Indira Gandhi helped break one of his hunger strikes over demands for affirmative action for Ladakh residents.
Wangchuk is married to Gitanjali J. Angmo, who also works in education, and has previously said he was prepared to die for his causes, including demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.
Pradhan has not responded to multiple emails from Reuters seeking comment.
Wangchuk earlier said his fast could last six weeks unless he died first:
"But hopefully we don't have to go that far. A sensitive government in a democracy listens to the pain of its people, and I hope they will take action."
"Our demand for a resignation will bring some accountability to the system," Wangchuk told Reuters days before Delhi Police swooped in to move him to hospital.
"At least, this will help restore trust in democracy."
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Eight years is too long': Anwar orders review of promotion delays
Article:
BANGI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ordered a review of civil service promotion processes after receiving complaints that some officers had to wait up to eight years for advancement.
Anwar said the directive followed feedback from civil servants during engagement sessions held nationwide, where concerns were raised over lengthy delays affecting certain service grades.
He said Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar had informed him that the issue would be discussed at the next meeting to ensure promotions could be expedited.
"Yes, salaries have increased, but I have also heard complaints that officers in certain grades have to wait as long as eight years for promotion. I have received many complaints about this.
"I was informed that for certain categories, promotions could take up to eight years. The Chief Secretary to the Government said this matter would be reviewed at the next meeting.
"Any promotion process that takes too long must be expedited. That is the instruction I have given," he said.
He was speaking at an engagement session with civil servants from the Central Zone at Dewan Sultan Idris Shah, Universiti Islam Selangor, here today.
Also present were Shamsul Azri, Public Service Department (PSD) director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz and Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari.
Anwar said the review was necessary to improve the welfare and motivation of civil servants while strengthening their commitment to delivering quality public services under the Malaysia Madani agenda.
He said that after taking office, his government found housing facilities for civil servants, teachers, Armed Forces personnel and police to be in poor condition, prompting gradual improvements through allocations beginning with Budget 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang Port key logistics gateway to Thai border
Article:
JELEBU: Penang Port continues to play an important role as a logistics gateway for southern Thailand by handling container and cargo movements between the neighbouring country and Malaysia, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
He said the port is handling a large number of containers to and from southern Thailand, thus strengthening its role as a regional logistics hub.
"We do handle a lot of containers to and from Thailand, so Penang Port is now a gateway to southern Thailand.
"The cargo is transported by containers via train from southern Thailand to Penang Port and vice versa," he said after meeting residents in Pekan Titi here yesterday.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim recently asked the Transport Ministry to discuss the use of ports on the Malaysia-Thailand border as well as in Penang to strengthen the development of infrastructure and logistics networks.
Anwar said transportation were among the matters discussed with his Thai counterpart Anutin Charnvirakul during the leader's recent official visit to Malaysia.
Meanwhile, Loke said the effort was not new because it was being actively implemented, including the development of the Perlis Inland Port (PIP) as a support centre for logistics operations.
"PIP is being developed as a place for storage, warehousing and container storage."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Smoked duck traders eye sales surge in Kuala Pilah
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Traders selling smoked duck in Kampung Terachi are hoping the two-week campaign period for the Negri Sembilan election will bring a surge in business.
Smoked duck is among the area's signature products, attracting customers from within and outside the state.
Smoked duck trader Khairul Hafiq Kamarul Zaman, 26, said sales typically surge in the three days leading up to polling day, based on the trend observed during the 2023 state election.
"On normal days, I usually sell only five or six smoked ducks.
"But during the state election, I expect sales to increase, especially in the three days before polling day.
"Based on the previous election, I was able to sell up to 40 ducks a day in the run-up to polling day," he said when met at Kampung Terachi.
Khairul Hafiq, who began helping his family sell smoked duck at the age of 15, said most of his customers were voters returning from Kuala Lumpur.
"Many of those who come to buy are from Kuala Lumpur because they are returning home to vote," he said.
He said his smoked ducks were priced between RM45 and RM48 each.
"The standard price is RM45 each, while the largest ducks are sold for RM48 each," he said.
Another trader, Mohamad Shafie Jahaluddin, 46, shared a similar view, saying sales were expected to increase ahead of polling day as many voters working in Kuala Lumpur returned to cast their ballots.
Besides returning voters, he said party members and election workers were also among his customers.
"I expect sales to increase because voters working in Kuala Lumpur will be coming home to vote," said Shafie, who has been in the smoked duck business for 22 years.
He said the smoked ducks he sold were priced according to their size and grade.
"The prices range from RM45 to RM70 each, depending on the size and grade," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US strikes Iran for ninth day, Iran immobilises two oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz
Article:
DUBAI: US forces hit Iran for a ninth consecutive day on Monday as concerns grew over shipping through the Strait of Hormuz after Iran said two oil tankers had exploded and been immobilised.
In a statement, US Central Command said it had begun "a new wave of strikes" aimed at "degrading" Iran's ability to attack commercial vessels in the strait's vital shipping lanes. It gave no further details.
The strikes are part of an escalating cycle of attacks between the US and Iran after an interim ceasefire agreement signed a month ago unravelled, deepening a struggle for control over the Strait of Hormuz that has disrupted energy supplies and stoked fears of global inflation.
Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency, citing its own reporters, said US missiles struck several Iranian cities early on Monday.
Explosions were heard in Tabriz, Chabahar, Konarak, Bandar Mahshahr and Bandar Imam Khomeini, according to Tasnim.
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the United States would continue to target Iran while it attacked global commercial shipping.
"The Strait of Hormuz are international waterways, and they continue to launch against the ships in that international waterway," Rubio said.
"As long as Iran insists on controlling an international waterway, we're gonna have to respond to that. The United States always remains open to a diplomatic solution."
For its part, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced a surprise strike against "the enemy's special operations headquarters in Syria's Al-Tanf area."
The IRGC also targeted US aircraft at Jordan's Aqaba airport with ballistic missiles.
Iran earlier conducted a drone attack on US military assets and equipment at Kuwait's Al-Adiri camp and Ali Al Salem Air Base, Iranian state TV reported early on Sunday.
Kuwait's government said a desalination plant was attacked for the second day running, causing a fire, in what it called a direct strike on vital civilian infrastructure.
Concerns about the flow of energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz have persisted since the United States and Iran resumed open hostilities after accusing each other of repeatedly violating a ceasefire deal.
The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations said late Sunday that it received a report that a vessel was on fire not far from the Omani coast, although it had not verified a cause.
[[nid:1493233]]
The IRGC said on Monday that two oil tankers had exploded and been immobilised after attempting to transit what it described as an unsafe southern route through Hormuz, alleging the vessels had been encouraged by the US military to use the passage.
Reuters could not immediately verify the incident. The statement gave no details on the vessels' names, flags, crews or any casualties.
The IRGC said the waterway would remain unsafe as long as what it called US "aggression" in the region continued, warning that "this passage will not be safe for the transit of petrochemical products, nor even a single drop of oil and gas."
Four vessels made the transit through Hormuz on Sunday, down from eight the previous day, LSEG data showed.
At least three oil products' tankers and one Very Large Crude Carrier have entered the strait since Friday to load oil, the data showed.
Oil prices rose two per cent to more than US$90 a barrel on Monday.
In the latest American casualty reported in the war, the US military said on Sunday that a service member was killed on Saturday in northern Iraq, where the US has military bases, during a controlled detonation of what Central Command called unexploded ordnance on a downed Iranian attack drone.
The US military said earlier that two service personnel died in Jordan and that a third was missing in action.
On Sunday, Central Command said that "unidentified remains" had been found at the site of Friday's attack and that "an examination process to verify the remains is ongoing."
The latest casualty announcements brought the total number of US military personnel killed since the war began to 17, with more than 420 others wounded.
The conflict, which began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on Feb 28, seeking to disable Teheran's nuclear and missile programs and weaken its regional proxies, has killed thousands of people, mostly in Iran and Lebanon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tengku Ampuan Pahang offers land in Kuala Wau for displaced Orang Asli community
Article:
KUANTAN: The Tengku Ampuan Pahang has expressed concern over the land dispute involving the Orang Asli community of Kampung Sungai Cot and offered a new settlement on her land in Kuala Wau, Maran.
However, the offer was not specifically intended for the families of Kampung Sungai Cot, but was extended to other Orang Kita (Orang Asli) families requiring shelter or relocation, subject to Her Royal Highness's consent.
Comptroller of the Royal Household of the Sultan of Pahang Major General Datuk Mohamed Zahari Yahya said Tengku Ampuan Pahang Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah had been closely monitoring developments surrounding the Kampung Sungai Cot issue and understood the difficulties faced by the community.
"Her Royal Highness has expressed deep sympathy and concern over the land dispute affecting the Orang Kita community. They are not only an integral part of the people of Pahang but also hold a special place in His Royal Highness's heart.
"In the spirit of compassion and responsibility, Her Royal Highness has invited any Orang Kita families from Kampung Sungai Cot who require housing to relocate and reside in Kuala Wau.
"However, the invitation is not limited to villagers from Kampung Sungai Cot but is also open to other Orang Kita families who require shelter or resettlement arrangements, subject to Her Royal Highness's approval and in accordance with applicable laws and regulations," he said in a statement.
Zahari said Tengku Ampuan Pahang had assured the Orang Kita community that they need not worry if the land in Kuala Wau is developed in the future, as she would ensure that those residing there would continue to have a place to live and would not be marginalised.
"The Orang Kita community will continue to receive support and may remain living in the area. It is hoped the offer will provide relief to those affected while ensuring that their welfare continues to be protected," the statement read.
Zahari said that research undertaken by Her Royal Highness for her thesis had highlighted the important role of the Orang Kita community in the development of Pahang over many years.
"Therefore, the well-being and future of the Orang Kita community have always been among the matters given attention and consideration by Her Royal Highness," the statement read.
Meanwhile, Zahari said Tengku Ampuan Pahang had called on all parties involved to uphold the spirit of consensus, mutual respect and constructive engagement in seeking a fair solution that safeguards the interests of all quarters.
"Her Royal Highness hopes that ongoing and future efforts by all relevant parties will lead to the best possible outcome for the well-being of the Orang Asli community and the harmony of the state," the statement read.
On July 17, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he would raise the
Kampung Sungai Cot land dispute
with the Pahang government after a group visited his residence in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang, seeking federal intervention.
The community claimed that the land belonging to dozens of
Jakun families
was sold by the Pahang government to a developer in 2015, and that several homes had since been demolished or burned in attempts to evict them from their ancestral land.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt ready to take measures to protect people's welfare, economy amid geopolitical tension
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is prepared to take the appropriate measures to safeguard the people's welfare and sustain the country's economic growth following the renewed escalation of geopolitical conflict in West Asia, said Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah.
He noted that the United States-Iran conflict, which reignited on July 8, 2026, has caused global oil prices to rise, thereby increasing the risk of higher production input costs.
Hence, Mohd Shahar said, the government is closely monitoring the situation as it has the potential to put pressure on industrial production costs and the prices of goods, particularly if the conflict persists.
"The government has mobilised various strategic and integrated intervention measures based on four key approaches: ensuring supplies, prolonging supply availability, curbing price increases, and capitalising on available opportunities.
"These approaches are aimed at protecting the people, stabilising supplies and prices, providing support to firms and industries, and strengthening economic resilience over the medium and long term through targeted interventions," he said during the question-and-answer session in the Dewan Negara today.
He was responding to a question from Senator Datuk Mohd Na'im Mokhtar regarding the impact of trade disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz on the national economy and the government's contingency plans should the crisis persist.
Mohd Shahar said that to stabilise supplies and prices, the government is also strengthening bilateral cooperation based on the country's strategic needs, including with Australia which focuses on the continuity of energy and agricultural input supplies, while cooperation with Russia is focused on the energy, technology, and trade sectors.
"The government is also providing support to firms and industries through the RM5 billion Small and Medium Enterprise Stabilisation Relief Facility.
"The government is also enhancing economic resilience by accelerating the implementation of the National Energy Transition Roadmap, New Industrial Master Plan 2030, National Semiconductor Strategy, and Malaysia Digital 2030 action plan," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prysmian signs €5.5 billion Molex deal in data centre push
Article:
ROME: Italian cable maker Prysmian said on Monday it had signed a long-term agreement worth up to €5.5 billion (US$6.4 billion) with Molex, a Koch-owned electronics company, as it stepped up its push into the fast-growing data centre market.
The deal runs for up to 10 years and includes a €550 million upfront payment and covers the supply of optical cables used inside data centres, Prysmian said in a statement.
The agreement is part of a broader set of deals and commercial initiatives with hyperscalers and data centre infrastructure providers.
These deals could generate more than €10 billion in additional cumulative revenue by 2035 compared with 2025.
The initiatives are expected to deliver as much as €1.1 billion in annual revenue from 2031.
To support demand driven by AI infrastructure and data centre upgrades, Prysmian will more than double fibre capacity in the US
The company will invest €1.25 billion through 2031 to expand optical cable and fibre production in the US and Europe.
The plans will create more than 1,000 jobs globally, including about 600 in the US
Chief Executive Massimo Battaini described the investment and agreements as a "transformative moment" for Prysmian's Digital Solutions business.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palm oil gains on surging crude prices, El Niño worries
Article:
JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Monday, tracking a rally in crude oil pricesand strength in Dalian vegetable oils, while renewed worries about El Niño also helped pull prices higher.
The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange was up RM39 , or 0.85 per cent, at RM4,636 (US$1,134.33) a metric ton by the midday break.
"BMD CPO futures opened gap higher following bullish rally in energy prices and in CBOT Soy oil futures from Friday and in Chinese vegetable oil futures in Asian hours today.
Talks of a super strong El-Nino have once again started gaining traction, helping palm oil prices move higher," said Anilkumar Bagani, commodity research head at brokerage Sunvin Group.
Dalian's most-active soyoil contract gained 0.51 per cent, while its palm oil contract rose 1.16 per cent. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade increased 0.06 per cent.
Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.
Brent oil prices rose 2 per cent to more than US$90 per barrel on Monday, as escalating US-Iran hostilities in the Middle East restricted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.
Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.
Meanwhile, Malaysia is expected to see record high temperatures next year as El Niño strengthens, its meteorological department told Reuters, stoking worries about lower palm oil production.
The US Climate Prediction Center says El Nino has strengthened over the past month and is forecast to intensify through 2026 and continue through early 2027.
Palm oil may retest a resistance at RM4,613 per metric ton, a break above could lead to a gain to the July 9 high of 4,630, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea slides as AI selloff deepens; Gulf tensions cloud Asia outlook
Article:
SINGAPORE: Emerging Asian equities diverged on Monday, as a five per cent slide in South Korean shares underscored a deepening unwind in the AI trade, while an escalating war in the Gulf drove oil prices higher and revived inflation concerns across the region.
Shares in Seoul fell as much as 5.1 per cent as trading resumed after a market holiday on Friday. The benchmark KOSPI gauge has slumped more than 22 per cent for the month so far, confirming a bear market.
Taiwan's tech-heavy index rose as much as 1 per cent before paring gains, while the MSCI EM Asia equities index edged 0.5 per cent lower.
The South Korean and Taiwanese benchmark gauges together account for about 60 per cent of the MSCI equities index.
"Investors now are reassessing the sustainability of AI capex demand in the longer term, and Korea, which produces AI memory chips for data centres, are more sensitive to changes in market sentiment," said Kelvin Lam, senior China economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics, while noting that Taiwan's ecosystem is lesser exposed than South Korea.
Citi analysts cut Korea to "neutral" in their EM country allocation, from "overweight" since July 2025, citing volatile trading conditions even as the market continues to rank strongly in their fundamentals-based models.
Markets took a further hit on Friday after Chinese AI firm Moonshot unveiled Kimi K3, an open-weight model it said nears Anthropic's frontier Fable system, raising the stakes for this week's earnings from Alphabet, Intel and Tesla.
"This inevitably revived intense, DeepSeek-esque worries about a race to the bottom on model compute undercutting staggering investments by hyperscalers," said Vishnu Varathan, head of economics and strategy at Mizuho Bank.
In Southeast Asia, stocks in Jakarta rose as much as 1.2 per cent to their highest point since mid-June, while stocks in Manila clocked similar gains to hit their highest level since March 3.
Among currencies in the region, the Indonesian rupiah weakened to 17,991 against the dollar at open, before strengthening to as much as 17,960 later in the session.
Markets also turn their focus to Bank Indonesia's meeting due later in the week, with the central bank expected to raise rates again to stabilize the rupiah.
Elsewhere, the Thai baht weakened to as much as 33.70 per dollar, while Taiwan's dollar fell to as much as 32.436 per dollar, with both currencies hitting their lowest levels since late April.
The Thai baht has already been weighed down by low carry returns and a weakening current account, analysts at Maybank said, adding that they expect the currency to remain among the region's weaker performers this month given the lack of supportive catalysts.
The baht has fallen 1.2 per cent in July so far.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops debunk viral e-hailing kidnap claim
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Police have dismissed a viral social media claim alleging an e-hailing driver attempted to abduct a female passenger here, saying investigations found no evidence of any criminal offence.
District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the woman was believed to have exited the vehicle suddenly, although the reason for her actions remained unclear.
"The e-hailing driver, who is in his 40s, lodged a police report on July 15 to deny the allegation and assist in the investigation.
"He denied attempting to kidnap the passenger, and police investigations also found no elements of abduction," he said when contacted today.
The allegation circulated widely on social media, including WhatsApp and Facebook, warning the public about an e-hailing driver who was accused of attempting to abduct a female passenger.
The posts included the driver's photograph and vehicle registration number, urging members of the public to be cautious.
According to a police report shared by the driver's family, he had picked up the passenger at about 6.20pm on June 15 and was taking her to a factory along Jalan Bypass Kota Tinggi.
The report stated that the woman suddenly got out of the vehicle at the Taman Aman three-way junction while shouting for help, leaving the driver confused.
Several members of the public who witnessed the incident approached and questioned the driver.
Minutes later, the woman's family arrived and allegedly informed those present that she had a medical condition before settling the fare.
The driver's family claimed the viral allegation had damaged his reputation and affected his livelihood.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt cannot simply lower fuel prices further with RM40bil subsidy bill, says Anwar
Article:
KAJANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today defended the government's fuel pricing policy, saying Putrajaya already spends about RM40 billion annually on subsidies and cannot reduce pump prices further without jeopardising the country's fiscal position.
Anwar said calls for lower fuel prices often overlooked the scale of subsidies already borne by the government.
He said Malaysia remained among the few countries providing extensive fuel subsidies despite not being fully self-sufficient in petroleum production.
"As for fuel prices, I do not know why I keep seeing people on social media saying that I have not lowered fuel prices.
"People ask why we do not reduce fuel prices further. But who is going to pay for it?
"I do not understand it. They say fuel prices should be reduced to RM1.50 per litre, but when we first proposed RM1.50, petrol in Saudi Arabia was about 50 sen per litre," he said.
He made the remarks during an engagement session with civil servants from the Central Zone at Dewan Sultan Idris Shah, Universiti Islam Selangor, here today
Present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service Department (PSD) director- general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz and Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari.
Anwar said Malaysia continued to import almost half of its petroleum requirements, yet motorists still enjoyed RON95 petrol at RM1.99 per litre due to government intervention.
He said even major oil-producing countries were selling fuel at higher prices.
"In countries such as Saudi Arabia, petrol now costs around RM2.40 to RM2.50 per litre, even though they are among the world's largest oil producers.
"In Malaysia, every time you fill up your vehicle, there is a government subsidy involved."
The finance minister said the annual subsidy bill for petrol and diesel alone had reached about RM40 billion.
He said while the government remained committed to protecting the people from rising living costs, it also had to ensure public finances remained sustainable.
"We do not want to continue increasing national debt simply to reduce fuel prices further.
"We must strike a balance between helping the people today and ensuring the country's finances remain strong in the future."
In July 2025, Anwar announced that the price of RON95 petrol was reduced to RM1.99 per litre as fuel subsidies were rationalised.
Under the new mechanism, only Malaysians are eligible for subsidised RON95, while non-citizens and large companies will not be eligible for the subsidy and will have to pay the full unsubsidised price, estimated at RM2.60 per litre.
Anwar added that the government was also servicing substantial legacy liabilities inherited from previous administrations, including obligations linked to 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB), Felda and Tabung Haji.
He said prudent fiscal management had enabled Malaysia's economy to perform better than expected, recording 5.8 per cent growth, exceeding earlier official forecasts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man killed as car crashes into oncoming tanker in Kulai
Article:
KULAI: A 45-year-old driver was killed after his car veered into the opposite lane and collided head-on with an oncoming tanker along Jalan Johor Baru-Ayer Hitam last night.
The 11.30pm crash occurred at Km40 when the victim, travelling from Kulai towards Sedenak, lost control of his car.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Tan Seng Lee said the car entered the opposing lane before colliding with the oncoming tanker.
The victim was trapped in the wreckage and had to be extricated by Fire and Rescue Department personnel.
He was pronounced dead at the scene by Kulai Hospital paramedics.
The tanker driver escaped unhurt.
Tan said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
Those with information can contact the investigating officer Inspector Mohd Ruslan Mohamed at 013-3063710 or the Kulai traffic investigation and enforcement division.S
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar says Malaysia's debt ratio improving despite inherited financial burdens
Article:
KAJANG: Malaysia's debt-to-gross domestic product (GDP) ratio is improving despite the government continuing to shoulder billions of ringgit in legacy liabilities inherited from previous administrations, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said criticisms that the country's debt was continuing to rise did not present the full picture, as Putrajaya was simultaneously servicing major financial obligations accumulated years ago.
"I do not want to continue adding to the country's debt. Some say the government's debt is increasing.
"The reality is that the government debt-to-GDP ratio is declining, but the amount of debt repayment remains high because we still have to pay interest on previous debts," he said.
He made the remarks during an engagement session with civil servants from the Central Zone at Dewan Sultan Idris Shah, Universiti Islam Selangor, here today
Present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service Department (PSD) director- general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz and Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari.
He, who is also finance minister, said the government remained committed to strengthening the country's fiscal position without resorting to excessive borrowing, while ensuring inherited liabilities did not derail ongoing economic reforms.
Anwar said among the major financial commitments still being borne by the government were liabilities linked to 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB), Felda and Tabung Haji.
He said the government continued to spend almost RM1 billion annually servicing Felda's financial obligations alone.
"For Felda, we are paying almost RM980 million every year. Nearly RM1 billion annually.
"People ask why we keep talking about the past. But who is paying for it now? It is the government today."
Anwar said the financial burden stemmed from years of mismanagement, abuse of power and poor governance, citing decisions that had saddled government-linked entities with unsustainable liabilities.
He said the government was also continuing to absorb substantial subsidy costs, including around RM40 billion annually for petrol and diesel subsidies, while balancing demands for greater public spending.
"Every time people fill up their vehicles, there is a government subsidy involved."
Despite these commitments, Anwar said Malaysia's economy had continued to outperform expectations, with the country's latest gross domestic product growth reaching 5.8 per cent.
He said the figure had exceeded earlier projections by the Finance Ministry, Economy Ministry and Bank Negara Malaysia.
"This is a remarkable achievement. It is higher than what was projected in the budget and even above our latest forecasts."
Anwar credited the stronger-than-expected economic performance largely to the commitment and professionalism of the civil service, saying faster approvals, improved efficiency and better policy implementation had strengthened investor confidence.
He said while politicians formulated policies, it was ultimately the civil service that ensured their successful implementation.
"I may steer the government, but those who provide the support, commitment and energy are the civil servants.
"Without their dedication, these achievements would not have been possible."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heriot-Watt honours PNB Merdeka CEO Izwan Hasli, former Chief Justice Tengku Maimun
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Heriot-Watt University Malaysia (HWUM) has awarded Honorary Doctor of the University (DUniv) degrees on former Chief Justice Tengku Tun Maimun Tuan Mat and Datuk Izwan Hasli Mohd Ibrahim, chief executive officer of PNB Merdeka Ventures, during its 2026 graduation ceremony.
The university said the awards recognise their contributions to nation building, integrity and sustainable development.
The ceremony also celebrated the graduation of 309 students across two sessions.
Tengku Maimun was recognised for her distinguished service to Malaysia's judiciary and commitment to the rule of law and institutional integrity.
Accepting the honour, she dedicated the award to those who have worked to uphold justice and urged graduates to let their core values guide their professional journeys.
Izwan received the honorary doctorate for his leadership in sustainable property development and community-focused urban transformation.
He said development should improve lives and communities, adding that success should be measured by the positive impact created for society.
Heriot-Watt Pro-Chancellor Datuk (Dr) Nicol Ann David encouraged graduates to lead with integrity and resilience.
Provost Professor Mushtak Al-Atabi said adaptability and critical thinking would be essential in a rapidly changing world.
Principal and vice-chancellor Professor Richard A. Williams said universities play a key role in transforming lives and supporting society.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mechanic jailed 20 years, ordered seven strokes of the rotan for raping, robbing teen
Article:
PASIR MAS: A 23-year-old mechanic was sentenced to 20 years' jail and seven strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to raping and robbing a 15-year-old girl in Tumpat last June.
Muhammad Faryzuan Aiman Mat Yatim admitted to both charges before judge Zulkpli Abdullah.
On the first charge, he was accused of raping and injuring the teenager in a secluded, bushy area at Kampung Mesra Palekbang, Tumpat, at 7.40am on June 17.
The charge was framed under Section 376(2)(a) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison sentence of up to 20 years and whipping.
On the second charge, he was accused of robbing the victim of her Vivo Y19 mobile phone while armed with a wooden stick at the same location.
The charge was framed under Section 392 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 397 of the same Code, which provides for up to 14 years' imprisonment and a fine or whipping.
Deputy public prosecutor Siti Aisyha Nai'lah Harizan appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
Earlier, Siti Aisyha Nai'lah urged the court to hand down a sentence that reflects public interest and serve as a deterrent to the accused and the public.
She said the offence was serious and cruel, causing severe trauma and fear to the victim, who had no prior relationship with the accused and was dressed modestly in while exercising on the day of the incident.
In mitigation, Muhammad Faryzuan Aiman appealed for a light sentence, stating he had a wife and a three-year-old child.
The court sentenced him to 15 years' jail and five strokes of the rotan for rape, and five years' jail with two strokes of the rotan for robbery.
The sentences are to run consecutively, starting from the date of his arrest on July 7.
The court also ordered the accused to undergo counselling while serving his sentence and to be placed under police supervision for three years upon release.
It was previously reported that a 15-year-old girl was ambushed, robbed, and raped while jogging in Palekbang.
Passersby found the injured victim in a bushy area around 11am before taking her to the hospital for treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father fined RM3,000 after exposing toddler to drugs
Article:
PASIR MAS: A labourer was fined RM3,000 by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to exposing his 1-year, 8-month-old daughter to dangerous drugs, causing the toddler to test positive for methamphetamine and benzodiazepines.
Mohamad Shahrul Hisham Lailatulazhma, 27, entered the guilty plea before Judge Zulkpli Abdullah.
According to the charge, he exposed his daughter to dangerous drugs, resulting in the child's body containing methamphetamine and benzodiazepines.
The offence was committed at a house in Taman Rantau Mas, Rantau Panjang, at 10.30pm on April 4.
He was charged under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001, which carries a maximum fine of RM20,000, imprisonment of up to 10 years, or both, upon conviction. The court may also order the offender to execute a bond for good behaviour.
Deputy public prosecutor Siti Aisyha Nai'lah Harizan urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence, arguing that the victim was a young child who should have been protected by her father.
She said the accused had instead failed in his parental responsibility by exposing the toddler to dangerous drugs.
In mitigation, Shahrul, who was unrepresented, pleaded for a lighter sentence, saying he had repented and needed to support his family as his wife was unemployed.
He also told the court that his daughter had since recovered and was in good health.
The court sentenced him to a RM3,000 fine, in default three months' imprisonment.
Judge Zulkpli also ordered the accused to execute a RM2,000 good behaviour bond with one surety for one year and complete 36 hours of community service.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tekun disburses RM36mil to 1,500 entrepreneurs in Negri Sembilan as of June
Article:
SEREMBAN: Tekun Nasional has disbursed financing totalling RM36 million to about 1,500 entrepreneurs in Negri Sembilan as of June this year.
Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong said today that, at the national level, RM540 million has been disbursed to 25,600 entrepreneurs during the same period.
According to Sim, Tekun, established 30 years ago, has contributed significantly to the development of local entrepreneurs.
However, he said, any weaknesses or gaps in the delivery process must continue to be improved based on the actual experiences of entrepreneurs.
In this regard, he has set a target for decisions on Tekun financing applications of RM100,000 and below to be issued within seven days, regardless of whether they are approved or rejected.
"For micro-financing of RM20,000 and below, the Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Ministry is working towards a target of providing decisions on applications within 24 hours," he said.
Recently, Sim attended a programme with Tekun entrepreneurs in Seremban, which served as a platform to gather direct feedback regarding their experiences in applying for, receiving, and repaying financing.
Sim emphasised that efforts to expedite the approval process must be accompanied by efficient fund disbursement so that entrepreneurs can utilise the financing without having to wait too long.
Meanwhile, he stressed that Tekun financing applications do not require support or endorsement from any political leader.
According to him, every application must be evaluated professionally and transparently based on the applicant's eligibility, regardless of race, religion, or political affiliation, and without reliance on any letters of support.
"Regardless of whether entrepreneurs wear blue, green, or red, as long as they are eligible, their applications must be given fair consideration. We want to put an end to the culture where entrepreneurs are forced to seek out politicians or third parties to obtain letters of support," he said.
Sim also said that the government continues to expand financing access for entrepreneurs across all communities, such as through the allocation for the Indian Community Entrepreneur Development Scheme, which has been increased from RM30 million to RM50 million.
Also, the Indian Community Entrepreneur Financing Package, which provides a total of RM220 million in financing this year, he said.
He said efforts to strengthen local enterprises should not be viewed through a racial lens, as all local businesses compete as Malaysian companies on the international stage.
"If we want to succeed internationally, we must succeed together. If we fail, we fail together," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sarawak’s jet age begins as Abang Johari joins AirBorneo maiden lift‑off to Kuala Lumpur
Article:
KUCHING, July 20 — AirBorneo today made history with the commencement of its first jet operations through the inaugural Kuching-Kuala Lumpur flight.
Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg was among the passengers on the maiden flight, MY5005, which departed from Kuching International Airport at 1.20pm for Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA).
The flight marked AirBorneo’s expansion of operations to Peninsular Malaysia using a Boeing 737-800 jet aircraft following the Sarawak government-owned airline’s takeover of MASwings Sdn Bhd’s operations on January 1 this year.
In his speech at the launch ceremony of the flight, Abang Johari described the airline as an important ‘bridge’ connecting Sarawak on the island of Borneo with the rest of the world.
“When Japan was rebuilding itself after the war one of its first strategic moves was to establish Japan Airlines. Like Sarawak, Japan is an island so connectivity is extremely important.
“After establishing its national airline, Japan became an industrialised and developed nation ahead of many other Asian countries.
“Learning from that, it is important for us to connect our economy with the world and our trading partners. We must have our own logistics network through which we can export our goods and services,” he said.
On July 7, AirBorneo announced the opening of ticket sales for the Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route, offering twice-daily services between Kuching International Airport and KLIA Terminal 1.
AirBorneo has set one-way fares at RM375 for Economy Class and RM736 for Business Class, inclusive of airport taxes and fuel surcharges. — Bernama

Title: Sessions Court hears RM8m FashionValet transfer, Vivy Yusof and husband deny breach of trust
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Sessions Court here was told today that RM8 million was transferred from FashionValet Sdn Bhd’s bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd through two online transactions in August 2018.
CIMB Bank’s Taman Tun Dr Ismail branch Customer Service Manager Azizan Hassan, 45, testified that a review of 30 Maple’s bank statements revealed online deposits of RM5 million and RM3 million from FashionValet’s bank account.
“On August 21, 2018, there was a deposit of RM5 million from FashionValet Sdn Bhd with the transaction description, ‘REMITTANCE CR/ROC/PB ECP PAYMENT FASHIONVALET SDN BHD PB ECP PAYMENT FASHION VALET SDN BHD ADVANCES TO 30 MAPLE’. I also confirm that the account balance before the deposit was RM2,678,853.48.
“On the same day, there was another deposit of RM3 million with a similar transaction description from FashionValet. I also confirm that the account balance before the RM3 million deposit was RM7,678,853.48,” she told the court.
Azizan was reading her witness statement on the opening day of the trial involving FashionValet founders Datin Vivy Yusof and her husband, Datuk Fadzarudin Shah Anuar, who are facing charges of criminal breach of trust involving RM8 million in investment funds from Khazanah Nasional Berhad (Khazanah) and Permodalan Nasional Berhad (PNB).
The prosecution’s first witness also told the court that 30 Maple’s bank statements showed a withdrawal of RM10,213,537.28 from the company’s account on September 3, 2018.
She further testified that the same account recorded an inflow of RM5 million on Sept 3, followed by a RM5 million withdrawal on Sept 4, 2018, bearing the transaction description “Interim Dividend FADZARUDIN SHAH”.
Azizan was giving evidence during examination-in-chief by Deputy Public Prosecutors Noor Syazwani Mohamad Sobry and Haresh Prakash Somiah.
She also confirmed that Vivy and Fadzarudin, both directors of 30 Maple, were the authorised signatories for the company’s bank account.
Earlier, counsel Datuk M. Athimulan, representing the couple, informed Judge Rosli Ahmad that he would only be able to cross-examine the witness on Friday as he was on medical leave until Wednesday. The court allowed the application.
